steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has one main finite verb: 'analyzed.' The blank fills the slot between the subject 'researchers' and the prepositional phrase 'for a relationship between...' — it must introduce a participial phrase that modifies 'researchers' without creating a second finite verb competing with 'analyzed.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "had looked": past perfect finite verb — competes with 'analyzed' as a second main verb, producing a run-on. ✗
  • "looking": present participle — modifies 'researchers' as a participial phrase, leaving 'analyzed' as the sole main verb. ✓
  • "were looking": past progressive finite verb — same problem as A; two finite verbs with no conjunction. ✗
  • "looked": simple past finite verb — same problem as A and C. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
